title: Markets react as Labour looks at decoupling gas and electricity prices
snippet:
Reeves and Miliband say want to finally break the marginal pricing formula

The post Markets react as Labour looks at decoupling gas and electricity prices appeared f
ai_summary: Labour government considering decoupling gas and electricity pricing, which could fundamentally alter wholesale electricity market structure and pricing mechanisms that underpin tem's PPA and supply offerings.
ai_reasoning: This is a significant policy shift that would directly impact how electricity is priced in the UK market. While not tem-specific, decoupling gas from electricity pricing affects wholesale markets, customer pricing strategies, and competitive positioning. Warrants monitoring and potential strategic response, but not immediate action as it's still in exploratory phase.

title: Heat pump sector urges policy clarity to unlock non-domestic adoption
snippet:
Industry calls for clear strategy, lower electricity costs and stronger support to scale heat pumps

The post Heat pump sector urges policy clarity to unlock non-domestic
ai_summary: Heat pump sector seeking policy support and lower electricity costs for non-domestic adoption, reflecting broader I&C demand for cheaper renewable power solutions.
ai_reasoning: Background reading on market trends. Heat pump adoption among I&C customers is relevant to tem's addressable market and future demand drivers, but this article focuses on heat pump industry advocacy rather than electricity supply dynamics or tem's competitive position. Worth tracking as a sector trend but no immediate action needed.
